### @jansol — 1 reactions  
`👍 1`  ·  [link](https://github.com/zed-industries/zed/pull/20400#issuecomment-2466792412)

> > seems like linux uses transparency
> 
> They are separate options, macOS supports both "transparent" and "blurred" background appearances. Linux always supports "transparent" and *additionally* supports "blurred" on some setups (currently only on KDE Plasma when using the Wayland session, should get more widespread once compositors agree on how exactly it should work)

### @thedeveloper-sharath — 1 reactions  
`👍 1`  ·  [link](https://github.com/zed-industries/zed/pull/20400#issuecomment-2467363056)

> @dovakin0007 Right, it heavily depends on the background being colorful otherwise it is very hard to see that there is an effect. Got it.
